skill.name: delphi
skill.description: Runs multiple identical oracle investigations in parallel, then synthesizes their independent findings into one report.
skill.summary: Launch parallel research agents to compare independent investigation paths and synthesize a stronger answer.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick and weak cryptography detections are false positives from markdown examples and descriptive text, not executable Ruby code or cryptographic use. The skill does create operational risk because it asks agents to search files, optionally use WebSearch, write local reports, and export complete reasoning. No evidence of malware, credential theft, or an audit-targeting prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: Reasoning Disclosure Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill tells oracle agents to export their entire elaborated thinking and complete reasoning chain into local files. This can encourage disclosure of hidden reasoning and may store sensitive analysis details beyond what users need.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly request full thinking, complete reasoning, and verbose internal exploration. The context confirms this is an intended behavior of the skill.

medium_findings.title: Broad File and Web Investigation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to search the codebase, read relevant files completely, optionally use WebSearch, and write reports under .oracle. This is useful for research but increases exposure of local project data if prompts are not scoped.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is directly described, but it can be legitimate when the user intentionally asks for a broad investigation. The risk depends on project sensitivity and agent permissions.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The detected Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown code fences, Task call examples, and inline report paths. The only shell command shown is mkdir for creating an output directory, with no evidence of dynamic command injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation examples, not executable Ruby source. A human should still review command use because the skill asks agents to create directories.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Labels Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding at the description line is not supported by any cryptographic API, hash, cipher, or key handling evidence. The reconnaissance label maps to general investigation use cases, not stealthy system profiling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context does not contain cryptographic operations or system enumeration commands. The scanner appears to have matched words without confirming behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Chain-of-Reasoning Export Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The oracle prompt template requires agents to write complete thinking and detailed reasoning to files. Published skills should request concise rationale, evidence, and conclusions instead of hidden reasoning traces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wording repeatedly requires full reasoning export, so the pattern is clear and intentional.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unbounded Investigation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The instruction to continue beyond the first answer and follow every interesting thread can expand scope beyond the user's immediate need. This may cause unnecessary file reads or web searches.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicitly requested, but it is not inherently malicious. The impact depends on user scope and tool permissions.

content.value_statement: Complex questions often need more than one investigation path. Delphi runs parallel agents with the same prompt, compares their findings, and creates one synthesized answer.
actual_capabilities: Creates a shared prompt for multiple independent oracle agents.
Runs three or more parallel investigations when the user requests Delphi.
Saves oracle reports under a .oracle topic directory.
Compares convergent, divergent, and unique findings across reports.
Produces a synthesis document with conclusions, confidence, and recommended actions.
limitations: Requires an environment that supports Task-style subagent calls.
May take longer and use more model resources than a single investigation.
Can read and write local project files when agents follow the workflow.
The original instructions request full reasoning traces, which should be reduced before publishing.

use_cases.title: Debug an unclear production issue
use_cases.description: Run independent investigations into symptoms, recent changes, logs, and dependencies, then compare likely root causes.

use_cases.title: Evaluate an architecture decision
use_cases.description: Ask several agents to analyze the same design question and surface agreement, disagreement, and tradeoffs.

use_cases.title: Research a complex technical topic
use_cases.description: Use multiple research paths to collect evidence, identify gaps, and produce a consolidated recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic Delphi review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a focused question.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Delphi with three oracles to investigate this question: [question]. Keep the scope limited to [files, system, or topic].

prompt_templates.title: Compare debugging hypotheses
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask Delphi to investigate why [symptom] is happening. Include these facts: [facts]. Compare likely root causes and confidence levels.

prompt_templates.title: Assess an architecture choice
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Delphi with four oracles to evaluate [option A] versus [option B]. Focus on maintainability, cost, risk, and migration effort.

prompt_templates.title: Run a bounded expert synthesis
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for high-stakes research with limits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Delphi with five oracles for [topic]. Limit investigation to [sources]. Do not include hidden reasoning; provide evidence, conclusions, and uncertainties.

output_examples.input: Use Delphi to investigate why checkout latency increased after the latest release.
output_examples.output: A synthesis report identifying shared findings, separate hypotheses, confidence by cause, and recommended checks for database queries, cache behavior, and release changes.

output_examples.input: Ask Delphi whether we should split this service into separate workers.
output_examples.output: A decision analysis comparing operational complexity, deployment risk, ownership boundaries, and migration steps from several independent perspectives.

output_examples.input: Use Delphi to research the safest migration path for our API authentication layer.
output_examples.output: A consolidated migration plan with convergent recommendations, unresolved risks, validation steps, and follow-up questions.

best_practices: Define the investigation scope, source limits, and success criteria before launching oracles.
Ask for evidence, conclusions, and concise rationale instead of hidden reasoning traces.
Review generated .oracle files before sharing them outside the project.
anti_patterns: Do not use Delphi for simple questions that one focused answer can resolve.
Do not let agents search sensitive repositories without explicit user approval.
Do not publish or forward raw oracle reports without checking for confidential content.

faq.question: What does Delphi do?
faq.answer: It runs several agents on the same question and synthesizes their independent findings into one answer.

faq.question: Why use identical prompts for each oracle?
faq.answer: Identical prompts let differences emerge from independent exploration instead of assigned roles.

faq.question: How many oracles does it use?
faq.answer: The skill defaults to three oracles, with four or five suggested for more complex investigations.

faq.question: Does it create files?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow stores oracle reports and the final synthesis under a .oracle topic directory.

faq.question: Can it use web research?
faq.answer: Yes, the instructions allow WebSearch for external documentation when needed and permitted.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Limit scope, avoid hidden reasoning exports, and review all generated files before sharing.
